key_insight: Transfer learning plus a hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász loss makes BiSeNetV2 much more robust to reflective welding seams without changing model size or speed.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lightweight, real-time perception upgrade for autonomous welding robots operating on shiny, low-contrast metal surfaces in construction.
method: Fine-tuned a BiSeNetV2 seam-segmentation backbone with transfer learning.
Replaced baseline optimization with a Cross-Entropy–Lovász hybrid loss to stabilize learning on difficult reflective cases.
Compared against BiSeNetV2, DeepLabV3+, UNet, and SegFormer under challenging welding imagery.
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline while keeping FLOPs, parameters, and inference speed unchanged.
watch_item: Results are promising, but deployment still depends on how well the model generalizes to new site lighting, surface finishes, and camera setups.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
